sources.outlet: PMC / peer-reviewed (ANTHC + Stanford symposium perspectives, published 2026-05-23) - primary
sources.note: Perspectives from the responsible-AI symposium on AI/ML in Alaska Native and American Indian (AN/AI) tribal healthcare systems; states that no ethical guidelines yet exist for AI/ML inside AN/AI tribal health, and that community involvement is almost absent from the healthcare-AI literature. This is the governance-gap the Dispatch is built on.

sources.outlet: PMC / peer-reviewed (community-engaged AI/ML framework, 2025-04-07) - primary, authored by the tribal health orgs themselves
sources.note: The community-engaged AI/ML framework that includes the medevac-decision model scoring patient vitals, weather, and aircraft status; frames data ownership and consent as prerequisites, not afterthoughts. Source for 'the community writes the guardrails before the model touches a patient.'

sources.outlet: International Journal of Circumpolar Health, Vol. 85 Issue 1, art. 2677426 (2026) - primary, peer-reviewed
sources.note: Documents the medevac-ML work growing out of a 2022 AIM-AHEAD-funded project (since awarded a second grant to expand), the 2025 symposium (31 participants), and the partner set (ANTHC, UAF Center for Alaska Native Health Research, UAA, Stanford).

sources.outlet: Stanford Emergency Medicine (EMED) - primary partner narrative
sources.note: Stanford EMED's account of the Alaska tribal-health collaboration, corroborating Stanford's role and the medevac decision-support framing.

sources.outlet: GovTech, 2026-06-09 (reprinting Anchorage Daily News, reporter Iris Samuels) - secondary context
sources.note: Alaska's Rural Health Transformation funding advancing 400+ projects; context for the state's rural-health / roadless-access backdrop (about 80% of Alaska communities are off the road system).
